items.body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.Provisioning.MySql` **Service directory:** `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/` **Triggered by:** commit 067f84057594a8976c95ad2138138ce139ae1130 (version increment after release PR #57985) by `@azure-sdk` ## What Changed `Azure.Provisioning.MySql` was published as its initial **1.0.0-beta.1** release on 2026-04-09. The package provides declarative provisioning support for Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Servers and includes the following generated resource types: - `MySqlFlexibleServer` - `MySqlFlexibleServerDatabase` - `MySqlFlexibleServerFirewallRule` - `MySqlFlexibleServerAadAdministrator` - `MySqlFlexibleServerBackup` / `MySqlFlexibleServerBackupV2` - `MySqlFlexibleServerConfiguration` - `MySqlFlexibleServerMaintenance` - `AdvancedThreatProtection` - `MySqlFlexibleServersPrivateEndpointConnection` - `MySqlFlexibleServersCapability` ## Gaps Found - **"Authenticate the Client" section is empty** — no content; comparable packages like `Azure.Provisioning.PostgreSql` also leave this blank, but for a first release targeting developers it should at minimum note that no client authentication is required (Bicep/ARM output is passed to `azd`) - **"Examples" section is empty** — no code showing how to create a `MySqlFlexibleServer`; the analogous `Azure.Provisioning.PostgreSql` README includes a full working example with SKU, version, admin credentials, storage, backup, and high availability configuration - **"Next steps" section is empty** — no links to Azure MySQL documentation, quickstart templates, or related packages - **No sample test file** — `tests/` directory contains only the `.csproj`; there is no `Samples/` subdirectory with a snippet-backed test, so the README examples cannot be kept in sync with the snippet tooling - **Key concepts section lacks resource inventory** — does not mention which resource types the package exposes or their relationships (e.g., `MySqlFlexibleServerDatabase` and `MySqlFlexibleServerFirewallRule` are child resources of `MySqlFlexibleServer`)
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files #### File 1: `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/README.md` — edit Replace the empty `### Authenticate the Client`, `## Key concepts`, `## Examples`, and `## Next steps` sections with the content shown below. **"Authenticate the Client" section** — replace the empty heading block with: ```markdown ### Authenticate the Client No runtime client authentication is needed for this library. `Azure.Provisioning.MySql` generates Bicep/ARM templates that are deployed to Azure using the [Azure Developer CLI (`azd`)]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) Credentials are handled at deployment time by `azd`, not by this library. ``` **"Key concepts" section** — replace the existing paragraph with: ```markdown ## Key concepts This library allows you to specify your infrastructure in a declarative style using .NET. You can then use `azd` to deploy your infrastructure to Azure directly without needing to write or maintain Bicep or ARM templates. ### Available resource types | Type | ARM resource | |------|-------------| | `MySqlFlexibleServer` | `Microsoft.DBforMySQL/flexibleServers` | | `MySqlFlexibleServerDatabase` | `.../databases` | | `MySqlFlexibleServerFirewallRule` | `.../firewallRules` | | `MySqlFlexibleServerAadAdministrator` | `.../administrators` | | `MySqlFlexibleServerConfiguration` | `.../configurations` | | `MySqlFlexibleServerBackup` / `MySqlFlexibleServerBackupV2` | `.../backups` | | `MySqlFlexibleServerMaintenance` | `.../maintenances` | | `AdvancedThreatProtection` | `.../advancedThreatProtectionSettings` | | `MySqlFlexibleServersPrivateEndpointConnection` | `.../privateEndpointConnections` | ``` **"Examples" section** — replace the empty heading with: ```markdown ## Examples ### Create a MySQL Flexible Server ```C# Snippet:MySqlFlexibleServerBasic Infrastructure infra = new(); ProvisioningParameter adminLogin = new(nameof(adminLogin), typeof(string)) { Description = "The administrator username of the MySQL Flexible Server." }; infra.Add(adminLogin); ProvisioningParameter adminPass = new(nameof(adminPass), typeof(string)) { Description = "The administrator password of the MySQL Flexible Server.", IsSecure = true }; infra.Add(adminPass); MySqlFlexibleServer server = new(nameof(server), MySqlFlexibleServer.ResourceVersions.V2024_12_30) { Sku = new MySqlFlexibleServerSku { Name = "Standard_D2ds_v4", Tier = MySqlFlexibleServerSkuTier.GeneralPurpose }, AdministratorLogin = adminLogin, AdministratorLoginPassword = adminPass, Version = MySqlFlexibleServerVersion.Ver8021, CreateMode = MySqlFlexibleServerCreateMode.Default, Storage = new MySqlFlexibleServerStorage { StorageSizeInGB = 20 }, Backup = new MySqlFlexibleServerBackupProperties { BackupRetentionDays = 7, GeoRedundantBackup = MySqlFlexibleServerEnableStatusEnum.Disabled }, HighAvailability = new MySqlFlexibleServerHighAvailability { Mode = MySqlFlexibleServerHighAvailabilityMode.Disabled } }; infra.Add(server); MySqlFlexibleServerDatabase database = new(nameof(database), MySqlFlexibleServer.ResourceVersions.V2024_12_30) { Parent = server, Name = "myDatabase", Charset = "utf8mb4", Collation = "utf8mb4_unicode_ci" }; infra.Add(database); ``` ``` **"Next steps" section** — replace the empty heading with: ```markdown ## Next steps - [Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Server documentation]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) - [Azure Developer CLI documentation]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) - [Azure.Provisioning overview](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/provisioning/Azure.Provisioning/README.md) - [Quickstart: Create an Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Server]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets 1. **Create the sample test file** at `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/tests/Samples/Sample1_MySqlFlexibleServer.cs`: ```csharp // Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved. // Licensed under the MIT License. using Azure.Provisioning; using Azure.Provisioning.MySql; using NUnit.Framework; namespace Azure.Provisioning.MySql.Tests.Samples { public class Sample1_MySqlFlexibleServer { [Test] [Category("Samples")] public void CreateMySqlFlexibleServer() { #region Snippet:MySqlFlexibleServerBasic Infrastructure infra = new(); ProvisioningParameter adminLogin = new(nameof(adminLogin), typeof(string)) { Description = "The administrator username of the MySQL Flexible Server." }; infra.Add(adminLogin); ProvisioningParameter adminPass = new(nameof(adminPass), typeof(string)) { Description = "The administrator password of the MySQL Flexible Server.", IsSecure = true }; infra.Add(adminPass); MySqlFlexibleServer server = new(nameof(server), MySqlFlexibleServer.ResourceVersions.V2024_12_30) { Sku = new MySqlFlexibleServerSku { Name = "Standard_D2ds_v4", Tier = MySqlFlexibleServerSkuTier.GeneralPurpose }, AdministratorLogin = adminLogin, AdministratorLoginPassword = adminPass, Version = MySqlFlexibleServerVersion.Ver8021, CreateMode = MySqlFlexibleServerCreateMode.Default, Storage = new MySqlFlexibleServerStorage { StorageSizeInGB = 20 }, Backup = new MySqlFlexibleServerBackupProperties { BackupRetentionDays = 7, GeoRedundantBackup = MySqlFlexibleServerEnableStatusEnum.Disabled }, HighAvailability = new MySqlFlexibleServerHighAvailability { Mode = MySqlFlexibleServerHighAvailabilityMode.Disabled } }; infra.Add(server); MySqlFlexibleServerDatabase database = new(nameof(database), MySqlFlexibleServer.ResourceVersions.V2024_12_30) { Parent = server, Name = "myDatabase", Charset = "utf8mb4", Collation = "utf8mb4_unicode_ci" }; infra.Add(database); #endregion } } } ``` 2. **Add the snippet placeholder** in `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/README.md` under the `## Examples` section heading, replacing the manually-written code block with: ```` ```C# Snippet:MySqlFlexibleServerBasic ``` ```` The `Update-Snippets.ps1` script will inject the actual code from the test file's `#region` block into this placeholder. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README currently has all required top-level sections present. The following are incomplete and need content as described in Step 1: 1. ✅ Getting started → Install the package — present 2. ✅ Getting started → Prerequisites — present 3. ❌ Getting started → Authenticate the Client — **empty** 4. ❌ Key concepts — **missing resource inventory** 5. ❌ Examples — **empty** 6. ✅ Troubleshooting — present 7. ❌ Next steps — **empty** 8. ✅ Contributing — present ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 mysql` — injects snippet code into README placeholder blocks; verify git diff shows README updated correctly 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 mysql` — only needed if any public API surface changed; skip if the sample file additions are test-only 5. `dotnet format sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/`
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
items.title: [MySql Provisioning] Docs: README missing authentication, examples, and key concepts for initial beta release
